title: The AI Bias Codex
subtitle: A Wikipedia-backed map of human judgment distortions in human-AI interaction.

quadrants.title: We trust AI too much or too little

statements.label: We over-defer to automated answers and system authority

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: We over-defer to automated answers and system authority
leaves.one_sentence_definition: The tendency to favor automated suggestions and ignore contradictory non-automated information.
leaves.interaction_rationale: The canonical page for over-weighting automated recommendations and discounting contradictory non-automated evidence.
cross_links: Judgment Steering by Anchors and Frames

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: We over-defer to automated answers and system authority
leaves.one_sentence_definition: The tendency to give extra weight to opinions or outputs associated with authority.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Explains why system labels, confident answers, and expert framing can trigger deference to AI output.
cross_links: Judgment Steering by Anchors and Frames

statements.label: Surface polish and credibility cues make AI feel trustworthy

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: Surface polish and credibility cues make AI feel trustworthy
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Information that feels easier to process is often judged as more credible or preferable.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Captures the shortcut where easy-to-process answers feel more credible, a common response to polished AI output.
cross_links: Judgment Steering by Anchors and Frames

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: Surface polish and credibility cues make AI feel trustworthy
leaves.one_sentence_definition: One positive surface trait can spill over and shape judgment of unrelated traits.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Explains how one positive surface trait, such as polished language or interface design, can inflate trust in the whole system.

statements.label: Once AI feels reliable, we monitor it less closely

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: Once AI feels reliable, we monitor it less closely
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Performance can decline when automation removes people from active control and monitoring.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Directly describes performance loss when automation is trusted enough that people stop actively supervising it.

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: Once AI feels reliable, we monitor it less closely
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Sustained attention is needed to detect infrequent signals over time.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Relevant because sustained monitoring deteriorates when AI handles routine tasks, making misses harder to catch.

statements.label: We resist AI when it feels controlling or threatens judgment

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: We resist AI when it feels controlling or threatens judgment
leaves.one_sentence_definition: The tendency to distrust or reject algorithmic advice, especially after observing mistakes.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Provides the clearest anchor for under-trust and rejection of algorithmic advice after observed error or perceived threat.

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: We resist AI when it feels controlling or threatens judgment
leaves.one_sentence_definition: A perceived threat to autonomy can trigger resistance to persuasion or control.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Explains resistance when AI recommendations feel controlling, autonomy-limiting, or imposed.
cross_links: Judgment Steering by Anchors and Frames

statements.label: Trust depends on calibration, oversight, and perceived control

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: Trust depends on calibration, oversight, and perceived control
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Trust is the willingness to become vulnerable based on expectations about another actor's behavior.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful background for trust calibration, but too broad to serve as a codex leaf on its own.

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: Trust depends on calibration, oversight, and perceived control
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Credibility is the perceived believability or trustworthiness of a source or message.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful for source-believability framing, but broader and less bias-specific than the accepted leaves.

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: Trust depends on calibration, oversight, and perceived control
leaves.one_sentence_definition: The perception and understanding of relevant elements in a changing environment.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Helpful for explaining lost oversight under automation, but not itself a human-AI bias concept.

leaves.quadrant: We trust AI too much or too little
leaves.statement: Trust depends on calibration, oversight, and perceived control
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Repeated experiences of lacking control can reduce initiative and independent action.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Relevant to dependence and reduced initiative, but more indirect than the core reliance pages.

quadrants.title: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
quadrants.failure_point: how our prior beliefs shape what we take from AI

statements.label: We interpret AI output in ways that protect prior beliefs

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: We interpret AI output in ways that protect prior beliefs
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People search for, interpret, and recall information in ways that support what they already believe.
leaves.interaction_rationale: The central concept for interpreting AI output in ways that confirm prior views and discount disconfirming material.

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: We interpret AI output in ways that protect prior beliefs
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Information is evaluated in ways that serve desired conclusions or identities.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Explains why people can use AI as a justification engine for desired conclusions rather than as a neutral check.

statements.label: We seek agreeable answers and overlook disconfirming parts

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: We seek agreeable answers and overlook disconfirming parts
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People tend to choose information that reinforces their existing views.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Directly captures the tendency to seek AI outputs that align with existing beliefs and avoid contradictory answers.

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: We seek agreeable answers and overlook disconfirming parts
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People more readily notice and remember information that fits existing expectations.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Relevant because people can notice agreeable parts of AI output while overlooking the parts that challenge them.

statements.label: We preserve beliefs by filtering evidence and examples

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: We preserve beliefs by filtering evidence and examples
leaves.one_sentence_definition: A belief can persist even after strong contrary evidence appears.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Explains why users may keep a belief even after AI or source material contradicts it.

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: We preserve beliefs by filtering evidence and examples
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Only the evidence that supports a preferred conclusion is selected while conflicting evidence is ignored.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Fits because AI can make it easier to surface only the snippets or examples that support a chosen conclusion.
cross_links: Judgment Steering by Anchors and Frames

statements.label: Recommendation loops keep feeding what already fits

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: Recommendation loops keep feeding what already fits
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Beliefs are amplified when repeated inside a closed, like-minded information environment.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Captures closed loops where repeated, like-minded AI-mediated content strengthens prior beliefs.

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: Recommendation loops keep feeding what already fits
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Personalized curation can isolate users inside a narrower information environment.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Explains how personalized ranking and curation narrow the range of views users encounter through AI systems.
cross_links: Judgment Steering by Anchors and Frames

statements.label: Repeated phrasing blurs memory, source, and certainty

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: Repeated phrasing blurs memory, source, and certainty
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Repeated claims can feel truer even when repetition adds no evidence.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful for repeated AI phrasing that starts to feel true, but narrower than the core accepted leaves.
cross_links: Judgment Steering by Anchors and Frames

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: Repeated phrasing blurs memory, source, and certainty
leaves.one_sentence_definition: A belief gains plausibility and spread through repetition and social reinforcement.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Helpful for social repetition effects, but more collective than the main belief-updating leaves.
cross_links: Judgment Steering by Anchors and Frames

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: Repeated phrasing blurs memory, source, and certainty
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People are quicker to detect bias in others than in themselves.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful for why users think others are misled by AI while assuming they themselves are objective.

leaves.quadrant: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
leaves.statement: Repeated phrasing blurs memory, source, and certainty
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People can misremember where a piece of information originally came from.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful for forgetting whether a claim came from the model, a source, or the user's own memory.

quadrants.title: AI's suggestions steer our choices
quadrants.research_title: Judgment Steering by Anchors and Frames
quadrants.failure_point: how AI presentation shapes our choices

statements.label: Initial suggestions and wording shift downstream judgment

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: Initial suggestions and wording shift downstream judgment
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Judgments are pulled toward an initial reference point or suggestion.
leaves.interaction_rationale: A direct fit for how initial AI suggestions, estimates, or ranges shift subsequent human judgment.

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: Initial suggestions and wording shift downstream judgment
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Decisions can change when equivalent options are presented in different ways.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Captures how equivalent AI outputs can lead to different decisions depending on wording and presentation.

statements.label: Defaults and option sets quietly push us toward one choice

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: Defaults and option sets quietly push us toward one choice
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People disproportionately stick with preselected or default options.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Relevant to model defaults, suggested prompts, and preselected actions that users tend to accept.

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: Defaults and option sets quietly push us toward one choice
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Adding an asymmetrically inferior option can shift preferences between the original options.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Fits AI-mediated option sets where adding one comparison shifts preference toward a target choice.

statements.label: What AI highlights feels most relevant and representative

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: What AI highlights feels most relevant and representative
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People judge importance or likelihood by how easily examples come to mind.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Explains why AI-highlighted examples or repeatedly surfaced cases feel more representative than they are.

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: What AI highlights feels most relevant and representative
leaves.one_sentence_definition: The tendency to notice some cues more readily than competing cues.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Fits interface-driven emphasis, ranking, and highlighting that steer what users notice first.

statements.label: Prompts and interface design cue decisions before reflection

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: Prompts and interface design cue decisions before reflection
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Earlier stimuli can influence later judgments or responses without conscious intent.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful for subtle cueing from prompts, examples, and system context that shifts later responses and decisions.

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: Prompts and interface design cue decisions before reflection
leaves.one_sentence_definition: The design of how options are presented can shape later decisions.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Provides the broad design framework for how AI interfaces structure the options users end up choosing among.

statements.label: Repeated exposure and vivid examples amplify interface steering

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: Repeated exposure and vivid examples amplify interface steering
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Behavior can be influenced by structuring the choice environment without removing options.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful for intentional steering language, but broader and more policy-oriented than the core leaves.

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: Repeated exposure and vivid examples amplify interface steering
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Repeated exposure can increase familiarity and liking on its own.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Relevant to repeated AI suggestions, but more indirect than the main choice-shaping concepts.

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: Repeated exposure and vivid examples amplify interface steering
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Easy-to-process information feels more familiar, trustworthy, or true.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful for polished output that feels right because it is easy to read, but it also overlaps strongly with trust calibration.

leaves.quadrant: AI's suggestions steer our choices
leaves.statement: Repeated exposure and vivid examples amplify interface steering
leaves.one_sentence_definition: A vivid individual case can evoke stronger judgment than abstract statistics.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful for vivid AI-generated cases that skew judgment, but narrower than the core design-level effects.

quadrants.title: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
quadrants.failure_point: how we misunderstand what the AI is

statements.label: Human-like cues make AI seem more person-like than it is

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: Human-like cues make AI seem more person-like than it is
leaves.one_sentence_definition: The attribution of human traits, motives, or feelings to non-human entities.
leaves.interaction_rationale: The foundational concept for attributing human traits, motives, or feelings to non-human systems.

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: Human-like cues make AI seem more person-like than it is
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Human-like feelings, intentions, or behavioral traits are attributed specifically to AI systems.
leaves.interaction_rationale: The most direct page for applying anthropomorphic attribution specifically to AI systems.

statements.label: Conversation triggers social and empathic over-attribution

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: Conversation triggers social and empathic over-attribution
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People can attribute understanding or empathy to a conversational program beyond what it warrants.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Directly explains why conversational fluency can make users over-attribute understanding or empathy to AI.

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: Conversation triggers social and empathic over-attribution
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People often apply human social rules and expectations to computers and software agents.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Captures the HCI finding that people automatically apply social heuristics to computers and agents.

statements.label: We read goals, beliefs, and intentions into system behavior

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: We read goals, beliefs, and intentions into system behavior
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Behavior is interpreted by ascribing beliefs, goals, and intentions to an entity.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful for interpreting AI behavior as if the system has beliefs, goals, or intentions of its own.

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: We read goals, beliefs, and intentions into system behavior
leaves.one_sentence_definition: A person mistakes subjective judgments or interpretations for objective properties of the world.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Explains the error of projecting one's own interpretation onto the world, including onto model output and behavior.

statements.label: We overestimate how deeply the system actually understands

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: We overestimate how deeply the system actually understands
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People often believe they understand a topic more deeply than they actually do.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Fits cases where users think either they or the model understand a topic more deeply than is actually true.

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: We overestimate how deeply the system actually understands
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Subjective confidence is often greater than objective accuracy.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Relevant to overestimating the reliability or competence of AI based on confident presentation and successful past use.

statements.label: Presence, patterns, and mental models fill in what AI lacks

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: Presence, patterns, and mental models fill in what AI lacks
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Digital interfaces can create a stronger sense that another social presence is really there.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful background for why interfaces feel socially real, but broader than the core attribution effects.

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: Presence, patterns, and mental models fill in what AI lacks
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Meaningful patterns or agency are perceived in ambiguous stimuli where none may exist.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Helpful for agency-attribution analogies, but less directly about human-AI interaction than the accepted leaves.

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: Presence, patterns, and mental models fill in what AI lacks
leaves.one_sentence_definition: An internal representation of how a system works.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Useful background for mistaken beliefs about how AI works, but not itself a bias page.

leaves.quadrant: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
leaves.statement: Presence, patterns, and mental models fill in what AI lacks
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People overestimate how widely their own beliefs and choices are shared by others.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Possible fit for assuming AI outputs mirror what most people think, but weaker than the core capability-illusion concepts.
